name: Spirited Pumpkin Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F and place the rack in the center.
2. Coat the bottom and sides of a 9-inch spring form pan evenly with unmelted butter and set aside.
3. Mix dry ingredients (bread crumbs, sugar) in a medium bowl.
4. Drizzle melted butter over the dry mix and stir until slightly darkened and uniform.
5. Press crust mixture evenly on the sides and bottom of the pan and refrigerate for 5 minutes.
6. Bake the crust for 8-10 minutes or until slightly dry, then cool on a wire rack to room temperature.
7. Cut cream cheese into 1-inch chunks and place in a large mixing bowl.
8. Beat at medium speed until completely smooth.
9. Continue beating while slowly adding brandy.
10. Add pumpkin and mix well.
11. Continue beating while very slowly adding sugars, scraping the sides of the bowl as needed.
12. Beat until smooth, then add spices and blend well.
13. Beat whole eggs with yolks in a small bowl at medium speed until blended.
14. Add eggs to the cheese mixture about 1/4 cup at a time, beating well and scraping the sides of the bowl after each addition.
15. Pour batter into the cooled pecan crust and spread the top smooth.
16. Gently rotate the pan several quarter-turns to settle the batter.
17. Bake until the sides of the cake are well set and the center is just set, about 1 hour and 25 minutes.
18. Turn off the oven. Let the cake stand in the oven with the door propped open for 8 inches for 30 minutes.
19. Place on a wire rack away from drafts and let cool undisturbed until sides and bottom are completely cooled.
20. Remove the sides of the pan and refrigerate uncovered overnight or at least 8 hours.
21. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and refrigerate until serving time.
